reservedFeatures.summary: Escaping closure environment support after escape analysis.
Date, timezone, wall-clock, and monotonic-time adapters.
Source-required tagged Haxe dynamic values and operations.
Contained general exception frames after result lowering is ineligible.
Thread-safe exported status and error-detail boundary.
Precise non-moving tracing collector and exact roots.
Reachability-selected type and member reflection metadata.
Network address, socket, and error adapters.
Threads, synchronization, thread roots, TLS, and atomics.
Unicode scalar algorithms not feasible as direct or local specialized C.

selectionRoots.description: Requested only by allocator, array, and string native seed fixtures; generated Haxe cannot select it yet.

documentation.runtimeRationale: Unknown runtime sizes and caller-supplied allocator identity need one shared ownership and failure contract, but compiler lowering for that case is still unsupported.
documentation.directAlternative: Stack storage, fixed arrays, nonescaping spans, and bounded values remain direct C and never request allocation.
documentation.contract: Implements checked size arithmetic, explicit allocator identity, aligned allocation, failure-atomic resize, and move-only allocation owners.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: A program-local allocator is preferred when whole-program escape and size facts permit a narrower specialized owner.

features.summary: Hardened allocator ownership and failure contracts with hosted and custom native evidence.

selectionRoots.description: Requested only by the array differential and selective-package native fixtures; generated Haxe cannot select it yet.

documentation.runtimeRationale: Runtime-dependent capacity plus reusable allocator and element-lifecycle boundaries justify this shared seed only after future array lowering proves the need.
documentation.directAlternative: Compiler-known fixed arrays and nonescaping spans use direct C storage and bounds checks.
documentation.contract: Implements a move-only resizable unboxed buffer with deterministic checked growth, borrow invalidation, and optional typed element lifecycle callbacks.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: Closed element types and bounded capacities should use a program-local specialized helper when that is smaller and equally correct.

features.summary: Resizable contiguous unboxed storage with checked growth and optional typed element lifecycle callbacks.

selectionRoots.description: Reachable Sys.println with a compiler-owned String literal.
Reachable default trace with compiler-owned literal text and source prefix.

documentation.runtimeRationale: One narrow hosted service centralizes exact-length output, embedded-NUL handling, flushing, and status mapping without pulling in general strings or allocation.
documentation.directAlternative: The literal representation stays direct C, but ISO C has no expression that performs the required hosted write and flush side effect.
documentation.contract: Writes one valid length-delimited Haxe String value plus a newline to hosted stdout and reports write or flush failure explicitly.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: Inlining fwrite/fputc/fflush at each call site would duplicate platform and failure policy rather than specialize semantics.

features.summary: Minimal hosted length-delimited literal output with explicit write and flush failure status.

selectionRoots.description: Requested only by the independent native runtime ABI smoke fixture.

documentation.runtimeRationale: The linked runtime must answer for its own build, but this evidence helper is not a generated-program semantic dependency.
documentation.directAlternative: Generated C checks HXC_RUNTIME_ABI_MAJOR directly with a structural _Static_assert and needs no query call.
documentation.contract: Returns the packed internal hxrt version for independent native compatibility probes; generated Haxe cannot select this query.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: A program-local query would report the compiler's assumption rather than the linked runtime's version.

features.summary: Version query for the internal same-major runtime ABI, used only by native seed evidence.

selectionRoots.description: Selected only when another registered feature needs the shared types or ABI macros.

documentation.runtimeRationale: A single header is the smallest dependency that keeps independently packaged runtime slices ABI-consistent.
documentation.directAlternative: Runtime-free generated C includes the standard headers and direct C types it actually needs, so this feature is omitted.
documentation.contract: Defines the narrow C11/C++ foundation and internal ABI macros shared by every packaged hxrt slice; it owns no allocation, failure, or thread state.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: A program-local copy would duplicate the internal ABI and visibility contract in every selected slice.

features.summary: Shared C types, internal ABI version, and visibility/alignment macros for selected runtime slices.

selectionRoots.description: Selected transitively by a slice whose C boundary can fail, currently hosted io or native seeds.

documentation.runtimeRationale: The header is the smallest shared failure vocabulary for separately compiled runtime sources.
documentation.directAlternative: Direct generated C uses ordinary control flow when the complete operation and failure semantics can stay local.
documentation.contract: Defines the closed non-throwing status vocabulary returned by current runtime operations; it allocates nothing and stores no error state.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: Duplicating numeric status values in each program-local helper would make independently packaged feature boundaries disagree.

features.summary: Status value definitions used by selected runtime failure boundaries.

selectionRoots.description: Requested only by independent native seed diagnostics and smoke fixtures.

documentation.runtimeRationale: The helper is shared native evidence, not a fallback selected for generated Haxe.
documentation.directAlternative: Generated code branches on typed status values directly and does not need a name lookup.
documentation.contract: Maps every known hxc_status value to a stable diagnostic name without allocating or retaining state.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: A fixture could duplicate the switch, but that would stop testing the runtime's own status vocabulary.

features.summary: Symbolic status-name helper used only by native seed evidence.

selectionRoots.description: Requested only by the string differential and selective-package native fixtures; generated Haxe cannot select it yet.

documentation.runtimeRationale: Arbitrary runtime bytes, scalar indexing, ownership transfer, builders, and CString lifetimes need shared validated machinery when future lowering admits them.
documentation.directAlternative: Known literals, constant concatenation, and statically decidable string facts should remain direct compiler-owned C data.
documentation.contract: Implements runtime-value-dependent scalar UTF-8 validation/indexing, owned strings, builders, slicing, hashing, and explicit borrowed or owned CString conversion.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: Closed call sites should receive specialized local operations when their representation, lifetime, and operation set are statically bounded.

features.summary: Valid UTF-8 scalar primitives, owned construction, mutable building, and explicit CString conversion with native evidence.

selectionRoots.description: Selected transitively when a runtime operation consumes the compiler's direct literal representation.

documentation.runtimeRationale: The header only shares the three-field call-boundary layout needed by selected consumers such as io.
documentation.directAlternative: The literal bytes and length are emitted directly in generated C; no runtime source or constructor is used.
documentation.contract: Defines the allocation-free private hxc_string view used for compiler-owned valid UTF-8 literal storage, including embedded NUL bytes.
documentation.programLocalAlternative: The carrier itself is already the program-specific representation; cloning its ABI would make runtime consumers incompatible.

features.summary: Immutable valid UTF-8 literal carrier with explicit byte length and no allocation or object dependency.
